items.question: She ___ me the good news.
explanations: 'Said me' is wrong — a person after the verb needs 'tell'.
Correct! A person straight after the verb takes 'tell': 'She told me the news'.
'Said to me the news' is not natural word order.
'Telled' isn't a word; the past of 'tell' is 'told'.

items.question: He ___ that he was tired.
explanations: 'Told that' needs a person: 'told me that'.
Correct! No person after the verb takes 'say': 'He said that he was tired'.
'Told me that' would be fine, but 'me' isn't in the sentence.
'Say' is the base form; the past is 'said'.

items.question: Please ___ the truth.
explanations: 'Say the truth' is wrong — it's a fixed phrase with 'tell'.
Correct! The fixed phrase is 'tell the truth'.
'Say to the truth' makes no sense.
'Tell to' is never correct.

items.question: Did you ___ hello to your parents?
explanations: 'Tell hello' is wrong — the fixed phrase uses 'say'.
Correct! The fixed phrase is 'say hello (to someone)'.
'Told' is the past; the question needs the base form 'say'.
'Say to hello' has the words in the wrong order.

items.question: Correct this: 'He said me to wait.'
options: He said to me to wait.
explanations: Correct! A person after the verb + a command takes 'tell': 'He told me to wait'.
'Said to me to wait' is not natural for a command.
'Say me' is grammatically wrong.
'Said me' is incorrect.

items.question: Children love it when you ___ them a story.
explanations: 'Say them a story' is wrong.
Correct! 'Tell + person + a story': 'tell them a story'.
'Say to them a story' is not natural.
'Tell to' is never correct.

items.question: She didn't ___ a word all evening.
explanations: 'Tell a word' is wrong — the fixed phrase uses 'say'.
Correct! The fixed phrase is 'say a word': 'She didn't say a word'.
'Told' is the past; after 'didn't' use the base form.
'Said to a word' makes no sense.

items.question: Can you ___ me the time, please?
explanations: 'Say me the time' is wrong.
Correct! 'Tell the time' + person: 'tell me the time'.
'Say to me the time' is not natural.
'Told' is the past; the question needs 'tell'.

items.question: He ___ goodbye and left.
explanations: 'Told goodbye' is wrong — the fixed phrase uses 'say'.
Correct! The fixed phrase is 'say goodbye': 'He said goodbye and left'.
'Told me goodbye' is not the natural phrase.
'Tell' is the base form; the past is 'said' here.

items.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: She told to me she was happy.
She said me she was happy.
She told me she was happy.
She said me that she was happy.
explanations: 'Told to me' is wrong — no 'to' after 'tell'.
'Said me' is wrong — no person straight after 'say'.
Correct! 'Tell + person': 'She told me she was happy'.
'Said me' is wrong.
